questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Two particles are projected vertically upwards from the surface of the earth with velocities <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>v</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mo>=</mo><msqrt><mfrac><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>g</mi><mi>R</mi></mrow><mn>3</mn></mfrac></msqrt></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>v</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>=</mo><msqrt><mfrac><mrow><mn>4</mn><mi>g</mi><mi>R</mi></mrow><mn>3</mn></mfrac></msqrt></math> respectively. If the maximum heights attained by the two particles are <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>h</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>h</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> respectively, then calculate the ratio <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><msub><mi>h</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><msub><mi>h</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></mfrac></math>.
questions.solution:
Loss of KE= Gain in PE

⇒12mv2=mghRh+R

CASE I.

⇒12m2gR3=mgh1Rh1+R     ...(i)

⇒12m4gR3=mgh2Rh2+R      ...(ii)

Solving (i)

⇒13=h1h1+R

⇒h1=R2        ...(iii)

Solving (ii)

⇒23=h2h2+R        ...(iv)

⇒h2=2R

From (iii) and (iv)

⇒h1h2=R/22R

∴  h1h2=14=0.25
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A spherical hole of radius $\frac{R}{2}$ is drilled from a planet of mass $M$ as shown in the figure. If the gravitational acceleration at a point on the surface of the planet just above the hole is $\mathrm{N} \times 1.1 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2$, find the value of N . [Given: $G=6.6 \times 10^{-11} \mathrm{~N} \cdot \mathrm{m}^2 / \mathrm{kg}^2, R=6 \times 10^6 \mathrm{~m}, M=6 \times 10^{24} \mathrm{~kg}$ ]
questions.solution:
If there were no hole
$g_0=\frac{G M}{R^2}$
decrease in g due to hole (absent mass)
$\mathrm{g}_1=\frac{\mathrm{GM} / 8}{(\mathrm{R} / 2)^2}=\frac{\mathrm{GM}}{2 \mathrm{R}^2}$
effective $\mathrm{g}=\mathrm{g}_0-\mathrm{g}_1$
$\begin{aligned}
& =\frac{G M}{2 R^2} \\
& =5.5 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
